title: 万圣节前夜的谋杀
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\126-万圣节前夜的谋杀-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Olga Seminova, an exchange student, argues with Mr. Fullerton regarding a disputed codicil to the will of the deceased Mrs. Llewellyn-Smith. Olga claims that the mistress wrote the document herself, noting that Mrs. Llewellyn-Smith had previously required Olga to practice imitating her handwriting to maintain Victorian social standards for private correspondence. Olga asserts that a gardener named Jim and a cleaning lady witnessed the signing of the document.

However, Fullerton reveals that an expert handwriting analysis concluded the codicil was a forgery. This suspicion was initially raised by his associate, Small Cole, who noted the handwriting did not match Mrs. Llewellyn-Smith's older files. Fullerton believes Olga’s greed motivated her to create a will that excluded all relatives in favor of herself. Although moved by Olga's tragic history of loss, Fullerton maintains that the evidence is against her and advises her to "come clean" to avoid harsher consequences, suggesting she might receive probation as a first-time offender and a non-fluent foreigner.

In response, Olga expresses feelings of persecution and contemplates fleeing Britain using fake papers or disguises, claiming she has saved enough money to disappear. Fullerton dismisses this plan as childish, warning that an arrest warrant would find her. Following this exchange, Hercule Poirot arrives at an estate where he is informed that Mrs. Dreyfus will soon arrive. While waiting in the living room, Poirot observes a meticulously maintained garden filled with asters, chrysanthemums, and roses.
